hazard.summary: Retry-After is parsed into a typed AbuseRateLimitError field but omitted from the stringified error surface, hiding retry timing from logs, MCP tools, and AI agents that only consume error text.
signals: AI agents receive MCP tool errors as plain strings
agent cannot inspect underlying struct with errors.As
secondary rate limit retry duration silently dropped

constraints: Decision-relevant retry signals must survive serialization and stringification boundaries
If Retry-After is available in a typed error, externally visible error representations must preserve it
Agent/tool-facing errors must include enough timing information to avoid blind retry behavior
Logging and string surfaces must not discard fields required for downstream retry decisions
verification: Construct an AbuseRateLimitError with RetryAfter populated
Call Error() and verify the returned string includes retry timing
Confirm the string output contains a machine-readable or human-readable retry-after duration
Verify callers that only receive Error() output can determine an appropriate wait interval
Regression test that RetryAfter is not silently dropped when present
notes: This is not a missing Retry-After parsing bug. The retry signal exists in the typed runtime object but is hidden at the boundary where many downstream systems, especially MCP tools and AI agents, consume errors as plain strings. The reusable lesson is that typed correctness is insufficient when decision-making happens after serialization.

constraints: agent-facing errors must preserve decision-relevant fields
typed error fields must not disappear at tool/log boundaries
anti_patterns: typed field populated but omitted from Error()
plain-string tool output hides retry timing
downstream agent forced to guess backoff
